text.content:
Original PDF page 106

III

mythical
TECHNIQUES
Original PDF page 107

Original PDF page 108

Techniques of Old

T

o martials and half-casters–warriors, as
we call them–Mythical Techniques are the
ultimate power-up. They are ancient arts of
great warriors of old who fought powerful
archmages, divine celestials, and
indescribable horrors from beyond the veil.
These are the tools of the sword saints,
undying barbarians, and unholy assassins of history,
passed down from generation to generation, and now
within your grasp.

In the Universe of Strings, each Mythical Technique
is a storied art polished over the course of centuries.
They manifest as shapes of one’s soul, channeling a
stream of energy so powerful that only those who have
trained their body to the limit can attempt to perform
them. Most often, Mythical Techniques are the result of
a particular mortal’s determination and resolve. The
work of a hero who, out of necessity or conviction, broke
the mold of what is typically possible.
Mythical Schools

Much like spells, Mythical Techniques are divided into
schools. Mythical Technique Schools have a particular
flavor to them, and in our setting, a history behind each
discovery and master. If you are not playing in the
Universe of Strings, consider adapting the schools and
reflavoring the techniques to fit the lore of your game’s
Universe.

Technique Mechanics

Mythical Techniques are not as powerful as the storied
10th-level spells, but as is often the case with Warrior
features, they can be used more often. Some Mythical
Techniques don’t even require rest, and are always
available to those who have mastered them.

Each Warrior Class gains its Mythical Techniques at
specific levels, as detailed in its class descriptions.
Mythical Techniques are powerful abilities that only a
select few warriors ever achieve. They have certain
limitations:

+

You can only perform a Mythical Technique with a
weapon you are proficient with. Some Mythical
Techniques require a certain weapon or type of
weapon to be performed, specified in the Weapon
line of each technique. Techniques that don’t have
that line do not require a weapon to be channeled.
Techniques that say “Any” in that line can be
channeled with any weapon, unarmed strikes, and
natural weapons.

Unless otherwise stated, you can only perform one
Mythical Technique per turn.
+

Like spells, Mythical Techniques sometimes require
concentration. If your concentration is broken, the
effects of the Mythical Technique end. Some of your
Mythical Techniques require your target to make a
saving throw to resist the technique’s effects. The
saving throw DC is calculated as follows:

Mythical Technique save DC = 8 + your proficiency
bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your
choice).

Custom Mythical Techniques

We highly encourage you to develop your own Mythical
Techniques! These represent the legendary skills of
your setting’s finest warriors, and can also serve as a
means to give a particular character at your table their
signature ability. Players are encouraged to think, both
in the case of 10th-level spells and Mythical Techniques,
on what their own imprint in history would look like. Of
course, Mythical Techniques are comparatively easier to
add than apocalypse-level arcanisms. We recommend
sticking to the following principles when creating
Mythical Techniques.

Power. How effective is your Mythical Technique?
What effects that are already in the game is the Mythical
Technique trying to emulate? What is the closest ability
that resembles this Mythical Technique? A Mythical
Technique should resemble a spell between 7th and 9th
level, depending on its ease of use and cost.

Uses. How many uses does this Mythical Technique
have? Does it expend Legendary Points? How difficult is
it to perform? The more powerful a Mythical Technique,
the harder it should be to set up. A Mythical Technique
similar to an 8th or 9th-level spell should have either
limited uses, an LP cost, or take several turns to set up.
A weaker Mythical Technique, closer to a 6th or 7thlevel spell in power, can be used without expending
resources other than the action needed to channel
them.

Opportunity Cost
Take into consideration the opportunity cost of using a
Mythical Technique when balancing its power. A 22nd-level
Fighter using Reality Slash deals 15d6 Lethal damage across
a 300-foot line–an average of 52 Lethal damage to every
creature in it. That same Fighter, using their action to
perform a Multiplier Attack, might deal significantly more
damage to a single target. Reality Slash is the right call
when there are five enemies in a line, or when lethal
damage is needed to bypass a resistance.

Mythical Techniques are not always the strongest option,
but they allow for greater flexibility.

Original PDF page 109

Mythical Techniques

The following is a list of all Mythical Techniques
available to Legendary Characters, separated by
Mythical School. Class restrictions are not made
between schools, but between techniques themselves.

LP Costs. Each Mythical Technique lists a Base LP
Cost. A Base LP Cost of 0 means the technique can be
performed freely on your turn, without expending
Legendary Points. A Base LP Cost greater than 0
means the technique always requires that expenditure
to perform. Many techniques also list an optional LP
Cost, which unlocks additional functionality – most
commonly, the ability to use the technique as an Extra
Action at the end of another creature’s turn, or to
enhance the technique’s effects beyond their base
parameters.

Learning Techniques. Mythical Techniques are
learned through your class, as detailed in each class’s
description. Unless a Heroic Path, class feature, or your
DM explicitly grants you access to a technique outside
your class list, you cannot learn it. If you are building a
custom character concept that seems to call for a
technique from another class’s list, speak to your DM!
There’s always a way to make it work.

Mythical Technique Types

Each Mythical Technique belongs to one of the
following types, noted in its description. Certain types
have specific interactions with other effects and
features.

Strike. Techniques that immediately release, often
requiring an attack roll.

Mobility. Techniques that allow for extraordinary
movement or repositioning.

Infusion. Techniques that infuse a weapon you are
carrying with a powerful enchantment for a duration.
You can only have one Mythical Technique infusion on a
weapon at a time.

Channel. Techniques that require channeling or
preparation, often with a longer casting time.

Passive. Techniques that enhance the character
passively, without requiring activation.

Original PDF page 110

pages.name: p. 111 - Ichimonji is the most famous school of Mythical Techniques. It is a transla

text.content:
Original PDF page 111

Ichimonji is the most famous school of Mythical Techniques. It is a translation of the abilities of the Xildur themselves,

developed by the legendary Kensei of Satokai, who descended to the Broken Hells in order to learn from the
mysterious Zedekiah, Keeper of Eden. Ichimonji focuses on great build-ups of soul energy, and lightning-fast,
singularly focused releases–cuts and slashes, most often. Most techniques of this school are singular strikes of

Ichimonji

The School of the Singular Strike

unendurable power.

Soul Strike

Strike, Ichimonji

Weapon: Any melee weapon or unarmed strike
Classes: Fighter, Monk, Ranger, Rogue
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 2
Range: Weapon range
Duration: Concentration, up to 1 minute

You channel your soul into your weapon and release it
as a burst of white flame shaped like the strike itself.
Make a weapon attack against a target within range. On
a hit, you deal Soul damage with your weapon attack,
and the creature falls unconscious as its soul is expelled
from its body and cast into the ethereal plane. While the
soul is expelled, the body remains unconscious and
cannot be awakened. A creature that has Legendary
Points can expend 2 Legendary Points to resist this
effect.

An expelled soul appears in a space within 5 feet of its
body on the ethereal plane. It retains the same statistics
as its body and can act on each of its turns, but remains
in the ethereal plane and can only affect creatures and
objects there. At the start of each of its turns, the soul
can attempt to return to its body by succeeding on a
Charisma saving throw. On a success, the soul returns
and the creature regains consciousness. Damage dealt
to the soul is also dealt to the body, but damage dealt to
the body does not affect the soul. When body and soul
are reunited, the creature’s hit points become the lower
of the two counts. If the body is killed before the soul
returns, the soul is lost in the ethereal and cannot
return unless the body is resurrected. If neither the body
nor the soul has been destroyed after 1 minute, the soul
snaps back to the body.

Creatures with no soul–such as certain Constructs and
Undead–are unaffected by this technique. This
technique can hit a creature in the ethereal plane.

God’s Justice

Strike, Ichimonji

Weapon: Any
Classes: Fighter, Monk, Paladin
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 1 LP
Range: 240 feet
Duration: Concentration, up to 1 minute

You channel your soul into a powerful and inevitable
strike, marking a creature within range. For the
duration, whenever the marked target takes an action,
bonus action, or Extra Action, you can use your reaction
to teleport to an unoccupied space within 60 feet of it,
regardless of the distance between you–even across
planes of existence. When you teleport in this way, you
can make a single attack against the target with
advantage. On a hit, the target must succeed on a
Constitution saving throw against your Mythical
Technique save DC or be stunned until the end of its
next turn. If the target is immune to the stunned
condition, it is impaired instead.

The effect ends early if the target dies.
White Flame

Infusion, Ichimonji

Weapon: Any
Classes: Fighter, Monk, Paladin
Channeling Time: 1 bonus action
Base LP Cost: 0 LP
Range: Self
Duration: Concentration, up to 1 minute

You infuse your next weapon attack, unarmed strike, or
Multiplier Attack with the white flame of your soul,
burning with a light that strikes deeper than flesh. For
the duration, the next weapon attack or unarmed strike
you make deals soul damage instead of its normal
damage type.

pages.name: p. 112 - After learning of the Ichimonji school, which encompassed the ancient arts

text.content:
Original PDF page 112

After learning of the Ichimonji school, which encompassed the ancient arts of the Xildur gods, Zedekiah taught Kensei

her own Mythical Techniques. These, Kensei brought back to the material plane as the Techniques of the Dragon

Heart, a melancholic yet firm and determined art, focused on protection, and paradoxically, chaos. At its core, the

Ryuushinkan is akin to a primordial mother, a powerful embrace, and a vicious retort.

Ryuushinkan

The School of the Dragon Heart

Divine Parry

Channel, Ryuushinkan

Weapon: Any
Classes: Fighter, Monk
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 2 LP
Range: Self
Duration: 1 minute

You prepare an almost insurmountable blocking force,
able to stop even a God in its tracks. For the duration,
when you are targeted by an attack, a Mythical Technique,
or a spell or magical effect of 9th level or lower, you can
use your reaction to nullify the effect against you and all
creatures and objects of your choice within 15 feet of you.
Elsewhere, the parried effect works as normal. Once you
successfully nullify an effect in this way, the Divine Parry
ends.

LP Cost. When you nullify an effect completely, you can
expend 1 additional Legendary Point to redirect it toward
the creature that created it instead. The creature must be
within 120 feet of you. All DCs, damage calculations, and
effects remain unchanged when redirected in this way.
Garden of Eden

Channel, Ryuushinkan

Classes: Fighter, Monk, Paladin, Ranger
Channeling Time: 1 minute
Base LP Cost: 0 LP
Range: 15-foot-radius sphere centered on you
Duration: Concentration, up to 8 hours

You expand your soul outward, creating a peaceful
spiritual garden in a 15-foot-radius sphere centered on
yourself. You must not be disturbed during the channeling
time for the Garden to manifest–if you take damage or
lose concentration before the minute is complete, the
technique fails. You can choose to make the garden
visible, with sprouting ghostly vines and flowers, or
invisible, with only a barely noticeable shimmer at the
edge of the sphere. You can shape the Garden to
resemble something other than vegetation, provided you
find the apparition peaceful. Neither you nor your allies
can interact with the invoked phantasms.

Once created, the Garden requires you to remain at its
center in continuous meditation. If you move or are
moved outside the sphere, the technique ends. While
inside the sphere, you and your allies regain 10 hit points
at the end of each of your turns. You become aware of

everything inside the Garden and gain truesight out to a
range of 60 feet. While meditating at its center, you can
take a short or long rest without sleeping, remaining
always aware of your surroundings and cannot be
surprised.

Spells and magical effects of 9th-level or lower cannot
manifest inside the Garden or overlap with it unless you
allow them. Creatures of your choice within the Garden
gain a +5 bonus to their AC. The atmosphere inside is
comfortable and dry, regardless of conditions outside.

When a hostile creature enters the Garden, you can use
your reaction to make a number of attacks against it
equal to your proficiency bonus from the center of the
sphere, as if your weapon had a reach of 15 feet. These
attacks are made at advantage.

Once you use this Mythical Technique, you cannot do so
again until you finish a short or long rest. If you used it
to take a rest, you cannot use it again for the next 8
hours.
Lotus Barrier

Strike, Ryuushinkan

Classes: Fighter, Monk, Paladin
Channeling Time: 1 reaction, which you take when
you or a creature within range takes damage
Base LP Cost: 1 LP
Range: 30 feet
Duration: Until the end of your next turn

You conjure a barrier of soul energy around yourself or
one ally within range. The barrier absorbs up to 200
points of damage and lasts until the end of your next
turn. While the barrier holds, the protected creature
cannot be forcefully moved, grappled, knocked prone, or
displaced, and is immune to effects that would reduce it
to 0 hit points or kill it outright. The barrier protects
against the triggering damage and any secondary effects
that would result from it, such as a poison or a magical
condition. If the barrier is destroyed, any damage
exceeding its remaining hit points is dealt to the
protected creature as normal.

LP Cost. You can expend an additional LP to target
one additional creature within range that is also affected
by the same attack, creating an additional 200 hit points
Lotus Barrier.

pages.name: p. 113 - Little is known of the Vorpal school. Its name is derived from the Blades o

text.content:
Original PDF page 113

Little is known of the Vorpal school. Its name is derived from the Blades of Vorpal, who would later be known as the
Unseen Blades in the kingdom of Iskendar. Legends tell, however, of an ancient murderer from the last Teng Kingdom

who was so effective in his craft that he channeled the essence of the God of Slaughter and brought ruin to the
Moonsilver Kingdom. Today, the Vorpal school is still practiced by the greatest killers all across the universe, often in

Vorpal

The School of Lethality

the service of mortalkind’s cruelest tyrants.

Cloak and Dagger

Mobility, Vorpal

Weapon: Any
Classes: Fighter, Rogue
Channeling Time: 1 bonus action
Base LP Cost: 1 LP
Range: Self
Duration: Concentration, up to 1 minute

You channel the power of your soul to slip between the
planes, vanishing from the material world entirely. You
enter the ethereal plane for up to 1 minute. While
ethereal, you have a fly speed of 60 feet, resistance to all
damage except psychic and force, and cannot be
perceived by creatures on the material plane. You can
move freely through the ethereal, observing the material
plane as you go.

At any point during the effect, you can use a bonus
action to choose a point you can see on the material
plane within 60 feet and tear back through the veil,
appearing in the nearest unoccupied space to that point.
Your first weapon attack or unarmed strike after
returning deals Lethal damage instead of its normal
damage type and has advantage.
Critical Edge

Infusion, Vorpal

Weapon: Any light weapon
Classes: Fighter, Rogue
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 1 LP
Range: Self
Duration: Concentration, up to 1 minute

You channel lethal nimbleness into your weapon,
infusing it with an edge that finds every gap in a
creature’s defenses. For the duration, the infused
weapon’s critical hit range is increased by 3.

pages.name: p. 114 - Of all schools of Mythical Techniques, none is more terrible than the schoo

text.content:
Original PDF page 114

Of all schools of Mythical Techniques, none is more terrible than the school of Blasphemy. Dainsleif is the name of a

sword now lost to time, forged by the best dwarven smiths for a Teng Hero, yet cursed by Malice to always cause a

mortal’s death when it was drawn. It simply couldn’t be unsheathed without causing tragedy. It ended up in the
possession of a lonely woman from a forgotten and ruined race, who hated the Teng, yet above all, blamed the Dead
Gods for her accursed life. Her name is forgotten, but her legacy of Blasphemy against Gods and Mortals still haunts

the Universe, in the form of the Blasphemous Techniques of Dainsleif.

Dainsleif

The School of Blasphemy

Blasphemous Hand

Strike, Dainsleif

Weapon: Unarmed strike
Classes: Barbarian, Fighter, Monk
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 0 LP
Range: 5 feet
Duration: Instantaneous

You forgo the need for a weapon, sullying your hand
with blasphemous energy. Make a single unarmed
strike against a target within reach. On a hit, your strike
plunges into the target’s soul, absorbing their essence.
The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw
against your Mythical Technique save DC or take 20d6
Dark damage. On a successful save, the target takes half
damage. You gain temporary hit points equal to the
damage dealt.

Hateful Blade

Infusion, Dainsleif

Weapon: Any melee weapon
Classes: Barbarian, Fighter, Ranger, Rogue
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 3 LP
Range: Self
Duration: 1 hour, or until dismissed

You infuse your weapon with an enchantment of Malice
and Hate, a blasphemous art that demands blood. For
the duration, weapon attacks you make with the infused
weapon deal maximum damage, and its damage type
becomes Dark. This infusion does not affect Multiplier
Attacks nor other Mythical Techniques. If you end your
turn without having dealt damage to a creature other
than yourself with the infused weapon, you take 10d10
Dark damage as the weapon turns its hunger inward. If
you kill a creature with this technique, that creature
cannot be returned to life by any means short of a 10thlevel spell.

One Use. Once you use this Mythical Technique, you
cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

Soul Wound

Strike, Dainsleif

Weapon: Any
Classes: Fighter, Ranger, Rogue
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 1 LP
Range: Weapon range/reach, up to 60 feet
Duration: Concentration, up to 1 minute

You make a single attack with the power to wound a
creature’s soul and deprive it of its most powerful
features. Make a weapon attack or unarmed strike
against a creature within range. On a hit, the target
must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your
Mythical Technique save DC or be unable to teleport,
leave its current plane of existence, use Mythical
Techniques, or cast spells of 8th-level or higher for the
duration. On a successful save, the target is unaffected.

If the target has no Mythical Techniques or spell slots
of 8th-level or higher, it cannot use its most powerful
action or feature for the duration instead. A dragon, for
example, cannot use its Breath Weapon. The DM has
final say on which feature this wound impedes.

A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of
each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

A Terrible Hell
It is said that those who are slain by the Dainsleif technique
are condemned to an eternity of torment. This is not true.
The souls of those destroyed by the Dainsleif are returned,
in time, to the White Flame, as shattered pieces of a whole.
There is no consciousness after the Death of the Soul.

Those who channel the Dainsleif, however, taint their soul
with blasphemy, and the hate of all who came before. It was
a mortal, not a God, who created these techniques,
channeling the powers of Rhox and Set into a red mélange
of murder and bloodshed. Unlike her legacy, her identity has
been lost to time. The Teng, perhaps in shame, ensured her
name would never be spoken again.

pages.name: p. 115 - Hundreds of years after the times of Kensei and Roland, in the Eastern land

text.content:
Original PDF page 115

Hundreds of years after the times of Kensei and Roland, in the Eastern lands of Kahar lived the Great Sultan AlMahdir of Bazandi, beyond the reaches of Uru and the Divine Lions. In his teenage years, the sultan encountered a
terrible creature on the road that offered him a most blasphemous deal. A Beast king, half-man, half-monster. After AlMahdir refused the creature, the Meratar cursed him with a malefice so ancient it dated to the Age of Gods. Al-Mahdir

spent his entire life creating an art capable of severing his curse, until, finally, on his deathbed, he discovered the
Zulfiqar technique of the ancient Xildur, capable of unmaking even the strongest of vows, promises… and curses.

Zulfiqar

The School of Severance

Divine Severance

Strike, Zulfiqar

Weapon: Any slashing melee weapon
Classes: Fighter, Paladin, Ranger
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 2 LP
Range: Weapon range
Duration: Instantaneous

You create a slashing attack able to undo the bindings of
divine laws, infernal contracts, fey curses, and even the
holy promise that spawned life in the universe. Make a
weapon attack against a target within range. On a hit,
the creature must make a Charisma saving throw,
taking 5d8 x 10 Soul damage on a failed save, or half as
much on a successful one. On a critical hit, you do not
multiply the damage dice by 2, but instead deal 8d8 x 10
Soul damage.

This attack slices through the soul, reducing the
target’s hit point maximum by an amount equal to the
damage taken. This reduction lasts for 24 hours or until
the creature is targeted by a greater restoration spell or
similar effect.

In addition, a creature that fails this saving throw by 5
or more must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw
against your Mythical Technique save DC or have a part
of themselves lost forever. Choose a non-vital body part.
The creature can no longer use that part, even if no
apparent wound appears on its physical form. The soul
of that body part can only be restored by healing magic
of 9th level or higher.

This attack can cut through anything. It can sever
promises, destroy magic, and expel divine will, creating
an effect similar to a 9th-level casting of dispel magic, or
other forms of severance – destroying unbreakable
chains, unmaking devil contracts, releasing souls from a
vow. The DM determines the extent of this effect in
each case. Some powerful effects may require more
than one strike to break apart.

One Use. Once you use this Mythical Technique, you
cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

Reality Slash

Strike, Zulfiqar

Weapon: Any slashing melee weapon
Classes: Barbarian, Fighter, Ranger
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 0 LP
Range: 300-foot long, 5-foot wide line / 90-foot cone
Duration: Instantaneous

You perform an attack so powerful it cuts through
reality itself. You can shape this attack as a 300-footlong, 5-foot-wide line or as a 90-foot cone. Each creature
in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking
15d6 Lethal damage on a failed save, or half as much on
a successful one. This attack affects creatures and
objects in the ethereal plane.

LP Cost. You can use this Mythical Technique at the
end of another creature’s turn by expending 1
Legendary Point.
Step of Disjuncture

Mobility, Zulfiqar

Classes: Monk, Ranger, Rogue
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 0 LP
Range: 120 feet
Duration: 1 minute

You channel your soul into an agile step that splits
dimensions, bestowing you with the energy of division.
You teleport to an unoccupied space within 120 feet
that you can see, leaving a fracture in reality in the spot
you previously occupied. At any point in the next
minute, you can use a bonus action to explode in a
scatter of force energy, dealing 35 (10d6) force damage
to all creatures within 30 feet of you and teleporting
back to the fracture. Once you do so, the fracture
disappears. You can only have one fracture active at a
time.

LP Cost. You can use the teleportation of this Mythical
Technique as a reaction to a creature targeting you with
an attack or spell, causing it to fail automatically if the
teleportation takes you out of the attack or spell’s range.
When you do so, you expend 1 Legendary Point.

pages.name: p. 116 - Elsewhere in the universe, around the same time as Kensei learned the techn

text.content:
Original PDF page 116

Elsewhere in the universe, around the same time as Kensei learned the techniques of Zedekiah, the legendary Hero
Roland of the Kingdom of Camadar charged against the hosts of the Storm King Ferrus in the battle of Roncev. Both

Roland and Ferrus met their end in that fateful confrontation, yet in their clash, a new school of martial techniques
was created, named after Roland’s sword, Durandal, and taught from generation to generation in the many successor

Durandal

The School of Iron

kingdoms of Camadar, most notably in Iskendar.

Earthbreaker

Strike, Durandal

Classes: Barbarian, Fighter
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 0 LP
Range: 100-foot-radius circle centered on you
Duration: Instantaneous

You channel the force of your soul through your legs
and into the earth itself, creating a tremor that rips
through the ground in a 100-foot-radius circle centered
on you. The ground in the area becomes difficult terrain.
Each creature on the ground that is concentrating must
succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your
Mythical Technique DC or have their concentration
broken. Each creature other than yourself on the ground
in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw
against your Mythical Technique DC or be knocked
prone. The tremor deals 50 bludgeoning damage to any
structure in contact with the ground in the area. If a
structure drops to 0 hit points, it collapses, potentially
damaging nearby creatures.

LP Cost. You can use this Mythical Technique as a
bonus action by expending 1 Legendary Point.
Unbreakable Stance

Channel, Durandal

Classes: Barbarian, Monk, Paladin
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 0 LP
Range: Self
Duration: 1 round

You bolster yourself with unbreakable might, flowing
from your soul to every last fiber of your body. You
regain 1 Legendary Point and become resistant to all
non-legendary damage except force, poison, and psychic
until the start of your next turn. For the duration, your
speed becomes 0 and you cannot be shoved or knocked
prone against your will. You must be standing on solid
ground to use this Mythical Technique.

LP Cost. You can expend 1 Legendary Point to instead
become immune to all non-legendary damage and
resistant to dark, force, lethal, poison, psychic and soul
damage for the duration.

Legendary Throwing

Passive, Durandal

Classes: Barbarian, Fighter
Range: Self

Your throwing arm carries the force of legend behind it.
The range of any thrown weapon you use is multiplied
by 100. When you hit a creature with a thrown weapon,
it must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your
Mythical Technique save DC or be pushed a number of
feet away from you equal to 5 times your Strength
modifier and knocked prone. A creature pushed into a
wall or solid surface takes an additional 5d10
bludgeoning damage. In addition, you gain the ability to
throw creatures and objects you are grappling an
incredible distance away. When you take the Attack
action, you can replace an attack with a Fling.

Fling. One Medium or smaller object held or creature
grappled by you is thrown up to 120 feet in a direction
of your choice and knocked prone. If a thrown target
strikes a solid surface, the target takes 10d6
bludgeoning damage. If the target is thrown at another
creature, that creature must succeed on a Dexterity
saving throw or take the same damage and be knocked
prone. Flinging a creature ends your grapple.
Obliterating Strike

Strike, Durandal

Weapon: Any heavy melee weapon
Classes: Barbarian, Fighter, Paladin
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 2 LP
Range: Weapon range
Duration: Instantaneous

You channel every ounce of your physical might into a
single, annihilating blow. Make a melee weapon attack
against a creature within reach. On a hit, the attack
deals 5d10 x 10 Lethal damage. On a critical hit, you do
not multiply the damage dice by 2, but instead deal
8d10 x 10 Lethal damage. A creature hit by this attack
must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against
your Mythical Technique save DC or be stunned for a
minute and pushed up to 30 feet away from you.

One Use. Once you use this Mythical Technique, you
cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

Original PDF page 117

Sunyata

The School of Emptiness

The origins of the Sunyata school are debated among
scholars. Some attribute it to the monks of the Rhazaali
jungles, others to the Yuna Monastery of the second
circle, where the ancient dragon of blue and red leads a
cult dedicated to strength of body and mind. What is
agreed upon is this: Sunyata is not a school of combat in
the traditional sense. It is a school of absence–of
clearing the mind until the self becomes a still core
around which the world moves. It claims dominion over
that which is or is not there, either to fill the space… or
to empty it.
Anatomical Control

Channel, Sunyata

Classes: Monk, Paladin, Ranger
Channeling Time: 1 minute
Base LP Cost: 0 LP
Range: Self
Duration: 1 hour

You enter a state of perfect anatomical awareness,
directing the flow of your body’s energy to heal wounds
from within. During the channeling time, you must
remain still and undisturbed–if you take damage or are
moved before the minute is complete, the technique
fails. Once the technique is channeled, you regain hit
points equal to half your hit point maximum, you are
cured of all poisons, diseases, and conditions affecting
you, and your hit point maximum is restored to its
normal value if it was reduced. You regenerate lost
limbs and lingering wounds and end any curse or
magical effect upon your body.

For the next hour, you continue regenerating,
regaining 20 hit points at the start of each of your turns
for the duration. This effect ends early if you are
incapacitated or if you drop to 0 hit points.

One Use. Once you use this Mythical Technique, you
cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.
Apparate

Mobility, Sunyata

Classes: Monk, Ranger
Channeling Time: 1 action
Base LP Cost: 0 LP
Range: 500 feet
Duration: Instantaneous

You focus your soul and body to modify your position in
space with only a thought, stepping through the void
between places. You teleport to an unoccupied space
you can see within 500 feet. You can bring one willing
creature within 5 feet of you along.

LP Cost. You can use this Mythical Technique at the
end of another creature’s turn by expending 1
Legendary Point.

Intervention

Mobility, Sunyata

Classes: Monk, Paladin, Ranger
Channeling Time: 1 reaction, which you take when
you or an ally within 60 feet is targeted by an attack or
spell
Base LP Cost: 1 LP
Range: 60 feet
Duration: Instantaneous

Where there is danger, there is a space that needs to be
occupied. When you or an ally within 60 feet of you is
targeted by an attack or spell, you empty your mind of
hesitation and appear where you are needed. You
teleport to an unoccupied space within 60 feet and
immediately take an action. You cannot use this action
to take the Recover action.
